steps.title: Identify where the sample came from
steps.content: The 40 students were randomly selected from a certain school, specifically from its fourth-grade students. This tells us which population the sample represents.

steps.title: Apply the generalization principle
steps.content: Random samples generalize to their source population only. Since this sample was drawn from fourth-graders at one specific school, results apply to all fourth-graders at that school—not to other grade levels or other schools, since those groups weren't sampled.

steps.title: Find the matching population
steps.content: Review the choices: A is just the sample itself. C is all students at the school, but includes non-fourth-graders who weren't in the sample. D is fourth-graders county-wide, but students from other schools weren't sampled. B is all fourth-graders at the school—exactly where the sample came from. That matches choice B — B is correct.
